scenario: Vertex SaaS migrates a Camunda 8.8 onboarding process used by 250 tenants to the new Camunda Process Test introduced in 8.8. The team must align every test class on a specific unit test framework version and choose the exact @Test annotation import so that the @CamundaSpringProcessTest engine starts correctly during the build pipeline.
question: Which JUnit version and @Test annotation import are required when writing Camunda Process Test cases in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Use JUnit 4 with the org.junit.Test annotation; the Camunda Process Test runtime in 8.8 also bridges legacy tests to the JUnit Jupiter engine automatically
Use JUnit 5 with the org.junit.Test annotation; the JUnit Jupiter runtime in 8.8 maps the legacy import to a Jupiter Test by default at startup time
Use JUnit 5 with the org.junit.jupiter.api.Test annotation; this combination is mandatory for every test class running Camunda Process Test in 8.8
Use JUnit 5 with the org.testng.annotations.Test annotation; Camunda Process Test in 8.8 standardizes on TestNG-compatible imports at runtime

a.text: Incorrect. The guide is explicit that Camunda Process Test was introduced with 8.8 and you must use JUnit 5; JUnit 4 is not the supported unit test framework for these tests in 8.8.

b.text: Incorrect. The @Test annotation that you import must be org.junit.jupiter.api.Test; the legacy org.junit.Test import from JUnit 4 is not accepted by Camunda Process Test in 8.8.

c.text: Correct. The guide states that you must use JUnit 5 in every test class and that the @Test annotation you import must be org.junit.jupiter.api.Test for Camunda Process Test.

d.text: Incorrect. Camunda Process Test relies on JUnit 5; TestNG annotations are not part of the documented setup for the @CamundaSpringProcessTest engine in 8.8 at all.

explanation: Camunda Process Test was introduced with Camunda 8.8 and requires JUnit 5 in every test class. The @Test annotation must be imported from org.junit.jupiter.api.Test, not from JUnit 4 or another test framework.
